reasoning: The base rate for a successful appeal of a CAS arbitral award to the Swiss Federal Tribunal (SFT) is exceptionally low, historically around 1–3% of all appealed cases. The SFT has very narrow grounds for overturning an award, primarily focusing on extreme procedural defects (like lack of jurisdiction or violation of the right to be heard/public policy). Although USA Gymnastics is arguing that they were given inadequate notice due to email errors and that the CAS chair had a conflict of interest, the SFT is notoriously strict on waiver rules. That is, if a party participates in an arbitration without immediately raising these objections, they are typically found to have waived them. The CAS stated that no objections to the panel were raised during the hearing. While the high-profile nature and significant procedural irregularities slightly elevate the chances of an overturn relative to an average case, the likelihood remains low. A probability of 9% balances the strong procedural claims with the extremely high burden of overturning a CAS decision at the SFT before the start of 2026.
